summary: High‑quality soundboard source that captures a blistering August ’68 show highlighted by an epic Alligator > Caution jam, soaring Dark Star and tight The Eleven.
review: The recording is a mostly stereo sound‑board capture with excellent clarity on the second set; occasional mono bleed and brief cuts in the first half keep it from being flawless. Musically the performance is lauded as one of the band’s early‑summer peaks – a monster Dark Star, fiery Alligator > Caution, tight The Eleven and a powerhouse Other One dominate the praise. Reviewers repeatedly note Jerry’s soaring leads, Phil’s aggressive bass duels and Bill’s drum fireworks, making this a must‑listen for 1968 Dead fans.

ai_rating.rationale: Over two dozen reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4–5 stars and cite standout jams; recording quality is praised as the best available source despite minor mono sections, yielding a strong combined score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Stereo sound‑board capture (256 kbps MP3) with very clear instrument separation on the second set. First‑set material is mono and shows a few clipping artifacts (St. Stephen intro) and brief cuts in Dark Star/Other One, but overall the mix is clean and lively.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A shaky opening (partial mono St. Stephen) gives way to a soaring second set where the band rides a wave of intensity, moving from The Eleven into a masterful Dark Star and then an explosive Alligator‑Caution sequence that culminates in a powerful Good Night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for endless runs, soaring solos (Alligator, Caution) and strong vocal presence; his tone is described as “full bloom” and “beyond amazing.”
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for aggressive, almost funky bass lines, dueling with Jerry in The Eleven, and a rare extended solo during New Potato.
band_member_comments.Keys: References to “perfect organ embellishments” (likely Pigpen/keyboard) during Death Don't; otherwise no explicit keyboard commentary.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming described as “going nuts,” with a notable drum solo in the Alligator jam and tight, energetic support throughout.
